proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_regorafenib (positive main effect on PFS).
Patients receiving treatment_cetuximab have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_cetuximab (positive main effect on PFS).
Patients receiving treatment_bevacizumab have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_bevacizumab (positive main effect on PFS).
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab (positive main effect on PFS).
Patients receiving treatment_encorafenib have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_encorafenib (positive main effect on PFS).
Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving it (positive main effect on PFS).
Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with stage_iv=0 (negative effect of metastatic disease).
Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative monotonic correlation).
Patients with right_sided_primary=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with right_sided_primary=0 (right-sided CRC has worse prognosis).
Patients with liver_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients without liver_mets.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 5.089 mo (treated, n=10022) vs 4.117 mo (untreated, n=39978). Difference +0.972 months, t-test p<1e-300. Strong positive main effect.
Mean PFS 4.285 vs 4.323; difference -0.038 months, p=0.089. No significant main effect.
Mean PFS 4.301 vs 4.320; difference -0.019, p=0.354. No effect.
Mean PFS 4.316 vs 4.311; difference +0.005, p=0.855. No main effect.
Mean PFS 4.316 vs 4.311; difference +0.005, p=0.873. No main effect.
Mean PFS 4.271 vs 4.315; difference -0.044, p=0.247. No main effect.
Mean PFS 3.703 (stage IV) vs 5.055 (non-stage IV); difference -1.352 months, p<1e-300. Highly significant negative effect.
Spearman rho=-0.360, p<1e-300. Strong negative monotonic correlation: ECOG 0=5.27 mo, 1=4.05 mo, 2=2.91 mo.
Mean PFS 4.114 (right) vs 4.419 (left); difference -0.305 months, p=5.2e-46. Right-sided primary has worse PFS unadjusted.
Mean PFS 4.296 vs 4.314; difference -0.019, p=0.516. No detectable effect of liver mets on PFS in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months specifically in KRAS/NRAS/BRAF wild-type patients (positive interaction), reflecting standard anti-EGFR biology.
treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months specifically in msi_high=1 patients (positive interaction), reflecting standard ICI biology in dMMR CRC.
treatment_encorafenib improves pfs_months specifically in braf_v600e=1 patients (positive interaction), reflecting BRAF-targeted therapy.
treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves pfs_months specifically in her2_amplified=1 patients (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: In RAS/BRAF wild-type (n=15376): cetuximab vs control diff=-0.045 mo, p=0.174. In RAS/BRAF mutant: diff=-0.034, p=0.252. No subgroup benefit; expected biology not observed.
In MSI-high (n=2513): pembrolizumab diff=+0.007 mo, p=0.955. In MSS: diff=+0.005, p=0.861. No benefit in target population.
In BRAF V600E (n=2272): encorafenib diff=-0.129 mo, p=0.359. In BRAF wt: +0.013, p=0.719. No benefit in target population (in fact, numerically negative).
In HER2-amplified (n=1504): trast/tuc diff=+0.017 mo, p=0.939. In HER2-wt: -0.046, p=0.234. No benefit in target population.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Comorbidity diabetes_mellitus is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Comorbidity heart_failure is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Comorbidity chronic_kidney_disease is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho=-0.0468, p=1.3e-25. Small but highly significant negative correlation between CEA and PFS.
Spearman rho=+0.1026, p=4.4e-117. Significant positive correlation; albumin is protective.
Spearman rho=-0.1201, p=5.6e-160. Significant negative correlation: greater weight loss predicts shorter PFS.
Spearman rho=-0.0074, p=0.096. Not significant.
Spearman rho=-0.0011, p=0.81. Not significant.
Spearman rho=+0.0015, p=0.73. Not significant.
Mean 4.302 vs 4.314; diff=-0.013, p=0.61. Not significant.
Mean 4.312 vs 4.312; diff=+0.001, p=0.98. Not significant.
Mean 4.340 vs 4.308; diff=+0.032, p=0.31. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months in this cohort (i.e., a positive correlation, atypical for oncology).
After adjustment for age_years, ecog_ps, and stage_iv, sex_female has no independent effect on pfs_months.
After adjustment for age_years, ecog_ps, and stage_iv, right_sided_primary remains independently associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho=+0.785, p<1e-300. Mean PFS by age band: <50=1.08, 50-60=2.65, 60-70=4.29, 70-80=5.95, 80+=7.78. Strong positive monotonic relationship, opposite to typical clinical expectation.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + sex_female + right_sided_primary', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS: sex_female coef=+0.0077 (p=0.41) - no independent effect of sex.

right_sided_primary coef=-0.285 (p<1e-100) in adjusted model. Independent negative effect of ~0.29 months persists after age/ECOG/stage adjustment. Age coef=+0.176/yr; ECOG coef=-1.18/unit; stage_iv coef=-1.35.

proposed_hypotheses.text: pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity groups (overall ANOVA test).
pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories (overall ANOVA test).
Patients with rural_residence=1 have shorter pfs_months than urban patients.
Higher smoking_pack_years is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher education_years is associated with longer pfs_months.
After adjustment for age, ECOG, and stage, race_ethnicity has no independent effect on pfs_months (refined null after seeing unadjusted ANOVA).

analyses.result_summary: ANOVA across race_ethnicity (white/black/hispanic/asian/other): F=0.872, p=0.480. Means range 4.27-4.41. No detectable difference.
ANOVA across insurance_type (medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured): F=1.475, p=0.219. Means range 4.25-4.33. No detectable difference.
Rural mean 4.331 vs urban 4.307; diff=+0.024, p=0.34. Not significant.
Spearman rho=+0.0028, p=0.53. Not significant.
Spearman rho=+0.0055, p=0.22. Not significant.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + C(race_ethnicity)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All race_ethnicity coefficients have p>0.34 (vs reference). No independent effect of race/ethnicity after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for age, ECOG, and stage_iv, treatment_regorafenib retains a strong positive independent effect on pfs_months.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_regorafenib and stage_iv on pfs_months (regorafenib effect differs by stage).
treatment_regorafenib improves pfs_months in left-sided (right_sided_primary=0) patients but not in right-sided patients (positive interaction with sidedness).
treatment_regorafenib improves pfs_months in KRAS wild-type patients but not in KRAS-mutant patients (positive interaction with kras_mutation).

Treatment assignment for biomarker-targeted therapies (cetuximab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, trastuzumab+tucatinib) is essentially independent of biomarker status (i.e., not enriched in target populations).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + treatment_regorafenib', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted regorafenib coef=+0.935 (p<1e-300). Effect grows after adjustment because regorafenib group is balanced and the unadjusted estimate was attenuated by other prognostic factors.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv * treatment_regorafenib', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: stage_iv:treatment_regorafenib coef=+0.029, p=0.179. No significant interaction with stage; regorafenib effect is similar in stage IV and non-stage IV.

analyses.code: stratified t-tests of regorafenib by right_sided_primary
analyses.result_summary: Left-sided (right=0): regorafenib diff=+1.480 mo (p<1e-300). Right-sided: diff=+0.026 mo (p=0.52). Strong qualitative interaction: benefit is restricted to left-sided primaries.

analyses.code: stratified t-tests of regorafenib by kras_mutation
analyses.result_summary: KRAS wild-type: regorafenib diff=+1.656 mo (p<1e-300). KRAS mutant: diff=+0.027 mo (p=0.45). Strong qualitative interaction: benefit is restricted to KRAS wild-type.

Pembrolizumab use: 15.3% in MSS vs 15.8% in MSI-high. Cetuximab: 30.3% in KRAS wt vs 29.9% in KRAS mut. Encorafenib: 10.2% in BRAF wt vs 10.7% in BRAF V600E. Trast+tuc: 8.1% in HER2- vs 8.0% in HER2+. Treatment assignment is biomarker-independent (no clinical enrichment).

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib improves pfs_months in BRAF-V600E wild-type patients but not in BRAF-V600E patients (interaction with braf_v600e).
treatment_regorafenib improves pfs_months similarly regardless of msi_high status (no interaction with MSI).
treatment_regorafenib improves pfs_months similarly regardless of her2_amplified status (no interaction with HER2 amplification).
In the simultaneously KRAS-wild-type AND left-sided subgroup, treatment_regorafenib produces a larger PFS gain than its overall main effect.
In a multivariable model that includes treatment_regorafenib*right_sided_primary and treatment_regorafenib*kras_mutation, both interactions are negative and significant.

analyses.code: stratified t-tests of regorafenib by braf_v600e
analyses.result_summary: BRAF wt: regorafenib diff=+1.027 mo (p<1e-300). BRAF V600E: diff=-0.156 mo (p=0.15). Benefit of regorafenib appears confined to BRAF wt (consistent with downstream MAPK pathway logic).

analyses.code: stratified t-tests of regorafenib by msi_high
analyses.result_summary: MSS: diff=+0.973 (p<1e-300). MSI-high: diff=+0.951 (p=4.5e-17). Comparable benefit; no interaction with MSI.

analyses.code: stratified t-tests of regorafenib by her2_amplified
analyses.result_summary: HER2-: diff=+0.976 (p<1e-300). HER2+: diff=+0.869 (p=3.4e-09). Comparable benefit; no interaction with HER2 amplification.
In KRAS wt + left-sided (n=18,801): regorafenib diff=+2.520 mo (p<1e-300), substantially larger than the +0.97 overall main effect. This is the favorable subgroup.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age + ecog + stage_iv + treatment_regorafenib*right_sided_primary + treatment_regorafenib*kras_mutation', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: treatment_regorafenib main coef=+2.129; interaction with right_sided_primary=-1.451 (p<1e-300); interaction with kras_mutation=-1.638 (p<1e-300). Both interactions ablate the regorafenib benefit roughly fully.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_bevacizumab does not significantly modify pfs_months in any of the major subgroups (sidedness, KRAS, liver mets, MSI).
treatment_bevacizumab differentially affects pfs_months in BRAF-V600E patients (interaction with braf_v600e).
treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months in left-sided primaries (positive interaction with right_sided_primary=0), reflecting classical anti-EGFR sidedness biology.
Among the 27 SNP variables, none show a clinically meaningful main-effect association with pfs_months (multiple-testing-adjusted null).

analyses.code: stratified t-tests of bevacizumab across subgroups
analyses.result_summary: Bevacizumab effect by right_sided_primary: -0.012/-0.034 (both p>0.27). By KRAS: -0.015/-0.020 (both p>0.49). By liver_mets: -0.020/-0.017 (p>0.38). By MSI: -0.024/+0.068 (p>0.26). No subgroup shows a benefit.

analyses.code: for braf in [0,1]: stratified t-test of bevacizumab
analyses.result_summary: BRAF wt: bevacizumab diff=-0.010, p=0.65. BRAF V600E (n=2272): diff=-0.237 mo, p=0.0067 - bevacizumab patients with BRAF V600E have shorter PFS. Marginal significance; could be chance with multiple comparisons.

analyses.code: for s in [0,1]: stratified t-test of cetuximab by right_sided_primary
analyses.result_summary: Left-sided: cetuximab diff=-0.049 mo, p=0.088. Right-sided: diff=-0.024 mo, p=0.49. No improvement in either group; expected sidedness benefit not observed.

analyses.code: for snp in snps: stats.spearmanr(df[snp], df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: Lowest p across 27 SNPs: snp_rs1801131 rho=-0.010 p=0.027 (does not survive Bonferroni correction with k=27, threshold p<0.0019). No SNP has a meaningful association with PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a comprehensive multivariable model, age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and cea_ng_ml each retain independent significant associations with pfs_months in the directions identified earlier.

In a comprehensive multivariable model, the effect of treatment_regorafenib remains highly significant and is concentrated in the (KRAS wt, left-sided) subgroup; the other five treatments contribute no independent effect.
prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with pfs_months (heavier-pretreated patients have shorter PFS).
Symptom grades (fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, appetite_loss_grade) are negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: All retained: age=+0.176/yr (p<1e-300), ECOG=-1.17/unit (p<1e-300), stage_iv=-1.36 (p<1e-300), albumin=+0.471/g/dL (p<1e-300), weight_loss=-0.077/% (p<1e-300), CEA=-0.005/ng/mL (p<1e-100). R^2=0.892. The age, ECOG, stage triad dominates.

analyses.code: same comprehensive model with all six treatments
analyses.result_summary: treatment_regorafenib coef=+2.145 (p<1e-300); regorafenib:right_sided coef=-1.473 (p<1e-300); regorafenib:kras_mutation coef=-1.640 (p<1e-300). cetuximab/bevacizumab/pembrolizumab/encorafenib/trastuzumab_tucatinib all p>0.07, |coef|<=0.022. Only regorafenib (in the favorable subgroup) is therapeutically active.
Spearman rho=+0.0064, p=0.15. No detectable effect.
fatigue_grade rho=-0.0019 p=0.67; pain_nrs rho=-0.0040 p=0.37; dyspnea_grade rho=-0.0064 p=0.16; cough_grade rho=-0.0043 p=0.33; appetite_loss_grade rho=+0.0027 p=0.55. None significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive three-way interaction treatment_regorafenib x right_sided_primary x kras_mutation on pfs_months: the negative two-way interactions partially cancel each other when both adverse markers are present.
The four-cell breakdown shows regorafenib benefit only in the (KRAS wt, left-sided) cell; the (KRAS wt, right), (KRAS mut, left), and (KRAS mut, right) cells show no benefit.
After adjusting for age/ECOG/stage_iv, individual metastatic site indicators (liver_mets, bone_mets, adrenal_mets, pleural_effusion) do not show independent associations with pfs_months.
BMI and vital signs (systolic_bp, heart_rate, spo2) have no detectable association with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction coef=+2.487 (p<1e-300). Decomposes as: regorafenib main +2.495; reg:right=-2.487; reg:kras=-2.503; three-way=+2.487. Net effect in (kras=1, right=1) ≈ 0; (kras=0, right=0) = +2.495; (kras=0, right=1) ≈ +0.008; (kras=1, right=0) ≈ -0.008.

analyses.code: stratified t-test in 4 cells of (KRAS x sidedness)
analyses.result_summary: KRAS=0 left=0: regorafenib +2.520 (p<1e-300, n_t=3763). KRAS=0 right=1: +0.071 (p=0.16). KRAS=1 left=0: +0.061 (p=0.17). KRAS=1 right=1: -0.040 (p=0.52). Confirms benefit cell.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age + ecog + stage_iv + liver_mets + bone_mets + adrenal_mets + pleural_effusion', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All site indicators p>0.07 with coefficients near zero (|coef|<=0.021). Site-of-mets information adds no independent prognostic value beyond age/ECOG/stage in this dataset.

analyses.code: spearman correlations of bmi, systolic_bp, heart_rate, spo2 with pfs
analyses.result_summary: BMI rho=-0.0061 p=0.17; SBP rho=+0.0060 p=0.18; HR rho=+0.0030 p=0.50; SpO2 rho=+0.0044 p=0.33. No association.
